Used with air hammer to drive valve guides. Page describes valve guide replacement procedure with heating cylinder head to 300°F (150°C) and chilling replacement guides. No torque specifications present on this page.

procedure_steps: heat cylinder head to 300°F (150°C)
chill replacement guides in freezer for 1 hour
drive guide 2 mm toward combustion chamber from camshaft side
turn head over and drive guide out toward camshaft side
cautions: Do not use torch; may warp head
Do not exceed 300°F (150°C); excessive heat may loosen valve seats
Wear safety goggles when driving valve guides
Hold air hammer directly in line with valve guide

This page contains idle speed adjustment procedures and specifications (550±50 rpm no-load, 700±50 rpm after ECM reset, 790±50 rpm with electrical loads). No torque specifications for fasteners are present on this page. The idle adjusting screw is turned by hand for adjustment, not torqued.

This page contains idle speed adjustment procedures and specifications, but no torque specifications for fasteners. The idle adjusting screw is turned to adjust idle speed, not torqued to a specification.

procedures: Check MIL, ignition timing, spark plugs, air cleaner, PCV system
Hold engine at 3000 rpm until radiator fan comes on
Connect tachometer or Honda PGM Tester
If engine stalls, restart with accelerator slightly depressed, stabilize at 1000 rpm, then release
Reconnect 2P connector to IAC valve, remove CLOCK RADIO (7.5A) fuse for 10 seconds to reset ECM
Restart and idle for one minute, check idle speed
Idle with headlights (Low) ON for one minute, check idle speed
Turn headlights off, idle with heater fan at HI and air conditioner on for one minute, check idle speed
meta.role_note: idle adjusting screw - not a torqued fastener, used for adjustment only

Check idle speed under various load conditions

reuse_conditions: A disconnected quick-connect fitting can be reconnected, but the retainer on the mating pipe cannot be reused once it has been removed from the pipe. notes: The fuel tube/quick-connect fittings assembly is not heat-resistant; be careful not to damage it during welding or other heat-generating procedures. The assembly is not acid-proof; do not touch it with a shop towel which was used for wiping battery electrolyte. When connecting or disconnecting, be careful not to bend or twist it excessively. Do not remove the retainer from the pipe; once removed, the retainer must be replaced with a new one.

manual_section: Emission Control System - Evaporative Emission (EVAP) Controls (cont'd)
procedural_notes: Check for a short in the wire (PTANK line): Measure voltage between the fuel tank pressure sensor 3P connector terminals No. 2 and No. 3.
Is there approx. 5 V? If YES: Replace the fuel tank pressure sensor.

If NO: Check for a short in the wire (PTANK line): 1. Turn the ignition switch OFF. 2. Disconnect the ECM connector D (16P). 3. Check for continuity between the fuel tank pressure sensor connector No. 3 terminal and body ground.
Is there continuity? If YES: Repair short in the wire between the fuel tank pressure sensor and ECM (D15).
If NO: Substitute a known-good ECM and recheck (see page 11-37 for immobilizer information). If normal fuel tank pressure sensor is indicated, replace the original ECM.

No torque values, fastener sizes, or special tools present on this page. Connector pinout table and wire side diagram shown for fuel tank pressure sensor 3P connector.

meta.section: EVAP purge flow switch malfunction - DTC P1459

content.description: The scan tool indicates Diagnostic Trouble Code (DTC) P1459: Evaporative emission (EVAP) purge flow switch malfunction.

troubleshooting_flowchart.step_1: Check if DTC P1459 is indicated
troubleshooting_flowchart.step_2: Check vacuum lines for poor connections, misrouting, breakage and clogging
troubleshooting_flowchart.step_3: Check the EVAP purge flow switch: Disconnect the 2P connector from EVAP purge flow switch. Check for continuity between EVAP purge flow switch connector terminals C20 and A10.
troubleshooting_flowchart.step_4: Turn the ignition switch OFF. Do the ECM Reset Procedure. Connect the SCS service connector. Start the engine. Hold the engine at 3,000 rpm with no load (A/T in N or P position, M/T in neutral) until the radiator fan comes on.

content.special_notes: Intermittent failure, system is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose wires at C292 (EVAP purge flow switch) and ECM.
